name: Natural Spot Peñaescabia — Circular from El Molinar
name_original: Paraje Natural Peñaescabia — Circular desde El Molinar
region_intro: Hiking around El Toro offers trails through varied landscapes, characterized by hills, natural springs, and forested areas. The region features a mix of open terrain and more sheltered paths, providing diverse hiking experiences. Elevation changes are present, ranging from gentle slopes to more significant ascents. The area is suitable for exploring natural spots and enjoying the local environment. Best hiking trails around El Toro The most popular hiking route is Natural Spot Peñaescabia — Circular from El Molinar, an easy 2.4 miles (3.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete, exploring a natural spot. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Molinar – Fuente de los Cloticos loop from Bejís, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This loop trail features natural springs and varied terrain, typically completed in 2 hours 55 minutes. Local hikers also love the Peñaescabia – El Molinar loop from El Molinar, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through natural landscapes and past El Molinar, often completed in about 4 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around El Toro is defined by natural springs, forested sections, and open hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in El Toro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore El Toro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Toro?
A: El Toro offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes traverse varied landscapes, including hills, natural springs, and forested areas, catering to different preferences and skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in El Toro?
A: When hiking in El Toro, you can expect a mix of open terrain and more sheltered paths through forested sections. The landscape is characterized by hills and natural springs, with elevation changes ranging from gentle slopes to more significant ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Toro?
A: Yes, El Toro features several circular hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879204063/natural-spot-penaescabia-circular-from-el-molinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Spot Peñaescabia — Circular from El Molinar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.4-mile trail. Another popular option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Molinar – Fuente de los Cloticos loop from Bejís&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.4 miles long and features natural springs.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in El Toro?
A: The hiking trails in El Toro offer a range of difficulty levels. While there are 3 easy routes, the majority of the 34 trails are moderate, with 28 routes falling into this category. There are also 3 more challenging routes for experienced hikers.

Q: What natural sights or landmarks can I see along the trails in El Toro?
A: Along the trails in El Toro, you can discover several natural monuments and points of interest. These include the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1756156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red earth&lt;/a&gt; formations, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6878568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscoja Oak&lt;/a&gt;, and various natural springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5660174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collao Spring&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Prao Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that feature natural springs?
A: Yes, natural springs are a notable feature of the hiking experience in El Toro. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Molinar – Fuente de los Cloticos loop from Bejís&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail specifically highlighted for its natural springs. You can also find springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5660174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collao Spring&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Prao Spring&lt;/a&gt; as highlights in the area.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in El Toro?
A: Hike durations in El Toro vary depending on the route and your pace. An easy trail like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879204063/natural-spot-penaescabia-circular-from-el-molinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Spot Peñaescabia — Circular from El Molinar&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescabia – El Molinar loop from El Molinar&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 4 hours 46 minutes for a 9.3-mile distance.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Toro?
A: The trails in El Toro are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the presence of natural springs, and the well-maintained paths through forested sections and open hills.

Q: Are there any facilities or picnic areas near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, there are facilities and picnic areas available. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palancar Station Campsite&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2614427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albentosa Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt;, which are convenient spots for a break or to enjoy a meal amidst nature.

Q: Can I find longer, more challenging hikes in El Toro?
A: While El Toro offers many moderate options, there are also longer routes for those seeking a greater challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescabia – El Molinar loop from El Molinar&lt;/a&gt;, at 9.3 miles (15 km) with significant elevation gain, is a good example of a more demanding moderate trail that takes nearly 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for exploring natural spots?
A: Absolutely. The region is ideal for exploring natural spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879204063/natural-spot-penaescabia-circular-from-el-molinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Spot Peñaescabia — Circular from El Molinar&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you through a beautiful natural area, offering an easy and enjoyable experience.
tips: It is a magnificent place to spend the day with the family, especially in summer as there is a lot of vegetation and some very nice bathing areas on the Palancia River. The recreational area has a water fountain, tables and benches, an open shelter and paella pans.
An old settlement that today is in ruins but has a very special charm and is surrounded by a wonderful natural environment next to the Palancia River.
